diagrams.title: Spanning Tree Example
diagrams.description: Shows a connected graph with 5 vertices and its spanning tree
elements: Spanning tree highlighted with 4 edges connecting all vertices without cycles
diagrams.purpose: Understand definition of spanning tree - all vertices connected with minimum edges (n-1)

diagrams.title: Cut Property Illustration
diagrams.description: A graph divided into two sets with minimum crossing edge highlighted
elements: Vertices split into Set S and Set V-S
Edges crossing the cut shown in different color
Minimum weight crossing edge indicated as part of MST
diagrams.purpose: Visualize why minimum crossing edge must be in MST - it provides cheapest connection between two partitions

diagrams.title: Kruskal's Algorithm Steps
diagrams.description: Step-by-step visualization of Kruskal building MST
elements: Edges being added one by one
Cycle detection shown when edge is skipped
diagrams.purpose: Understand the edge-by-edge greedy approach and cycle detection mechanism

diagrams.title: Prim's Algorithm Growth
diagrams.description: Tree growing from starting vertex showing cut at each step
diagrams.purpose: Visualize vertex-based tree growth and how minimum edge crossing cut is selected

diagrams.title: MST vs Non-MST Comparison
diagrams.description: Shows two spanning trees - one is MST, one is not
elements: First spanning tree with higher total weight
Second spanning tree (MST) with lower total weight
diagrams.purpose: Reinforce concept of minimum total weight in MST
